meta.purpose: Machine-readable fix list. Each item describes what is broken and what to do. Priority-ordered — fix from top to bottom.

fixes.action: Add JSON-LD structured data: LocalBusiness, Service, Review, AggregateRating, GeoCoordinates. NEVER use HowTo (deprecated Sept 2023). FAQ schema only has GEO benefit for commercial sites (no Google rich result since Aug 2023).
fixes.do_not: Don't add FAQPage schema expecting Google rich results on a commercial site — that was restricted in Aug 2023. The Q&A content structure still helps for AI citation though.

fixes.action: Restructure content sections to 134-167 words each, with statistics and source attributions. Each section should be extractable as a standalone answer.
fixes.evidence: 0.0% of sections are highly citable (93 sections analyzed)
fixes.do_not: Don't mechanically split every paragraph to exactly 150 words. Optimize the informational sections — leave testimonials, CTAs, and branding sections alone.

fixes.action: Add before/after project photos, customer testimonials with specific details, certifications/licenses page, and community involvement.
fixes.do_not: Don't fake experience with AI-generated case studies. Google's systems detect this. Use real client work, real numbers, real photos.

fixes.action: Add an FAQ section with 5-8 real questions your prospects ask. Each answer should be 40-80 words, lead with the direct answer, and include one specific number or data point. Q&A pairs are the most extractable content format for AI models.
fixes.evidence: FAQ content: found, Q&A headings: found
fixes.do_not: Don't write generic FAQs that nobody actually asks. Use real questions from sales calls, support tickets, and search queries. AI models can tell when FAQ content is filler.

fixes.action: Build presence on Reddit (~24% of Perplexity responses cite it), YouTube (0.737 correlation with AI citations — strongest signal), and LinkedIn (#2 most-cited domain in AI search — ALM Corp 2026). Most brand citations in AI come from third-party sources, not your own site.
fixes.evidence: {'reddit_presence': 'No Reddit presence found for PlAnhead brand', 'youtube_presence': 'No YouTube channel found for PlAnhead', 'linkedin_presence': 'No LinkedIn company page or personal profile linked from site', 'third_party_mentions': 'Minimal third-party coverage; references to VDI 2067, MSCI World, USDA/EFSA, ACSM, IANA give technical credibility but no independent editorial mentions found'}
fixes.do_not: Don't just create profiles and abandon them. Active participation (Reddit comments, YouTube content, LinkedIn posts) drives citations.

fixes.action: Add Open Graph image tags (og:image) to all pages. Create a branded OG image (1200x630px) for social sharing. Ensure og:title, og:description, and twitter:card tags are also present.
fixes.do_not: Don't use a generic stock photo. Use a branded image with your logo and a clear value proposition. Each key page should have a unique OG image.

fixes.action: Create /llms.txt at domain root — structured guide for AI crawlers describing your site, key pages, and value proposition.
fixes.evidence: llms.txt not found at domain root
fixes.do_not: Don't stuff keywords into llms.txt. It's for AI crawlers — be clear and factual about what your site offers.

fixes.action: Add visible links to your LinkedIn, YouTube, and Twitter/X profiles in your site footer and About page. Include these URLs in your Organization schema sameAs field.
fixes.evidence: No social profile links on site; Twitter card references @planhead_app but not linked on-page
fixes.do_not: Don't link to empty or abandoned social profiles. Only link profiles where you're actively posting — dead profiles hurt more than help.

fixes.action: Add named expert quotes with credentials. Use real quotes from team members, clients, or industry experts.
fixes.do_not: Don't invent quotes or attribute generic statements to unnamed experts. AI models check attribution consistency.

site_issues.detail: llms.txt: not found (no measurable citation impact as of March 2026)
No social profile links on site; Twitter card references @planhead_app but not linked on-page
18% of pages have visible update dates
No Reddit presence found for PlAnhead brand
No YouTube channel found for PlAnhead
No LinkedIn company page or personal profile linked from site
94.6% of sections have low citability and need restructuring
